name: eToro API Vocabulary
description: Terms derived from the eToro Public API OpenAPI specification

terms.description: Array of user profiles with their associated information
Global Customer ID - Unique identifier across all systems
Customer ID for real trading account
Customer ID for demo/practice account if available
Unique username identifier for the user
User's preferred language ID based on system language codes
ISO 639-1 language code for user's preferred language
User's registered country ID based on system country codes
Indicates whether the user has consented to displaying their full name publicly
Structured biographical information including trading strategy
White label partner identifier if user belongs to a partner program
Indicates if user has opted out of public profile features
Indicates if user is a Professional Investor with special privileges
User's current verification level (0-3, where 3 is fully verified)
Current account status code indicating active, suspended, or other states
User's first name (visible if allowDisplayFullName is true)
User's middle name
User's last name (visible if allowDisplayFullName is true)
User's full about me text
Short summary of user's about me text
List of customer restrictions applied to the account
ID of the user's trading strategy
Type of avatar image
Echoes the requested username.
Date of this snapshot (YYYY-MM-DD)
Cash/credit as a ratio of realized equity (cash + invested capital, excluding open P&L). Decimal fraction: 0.5 = 50%.
Cash/credit as a ratio of total equity (cash + invested capital + open P&L). Decimal fraction: 0.5 = 50%.
Per-instrument breakdown. Mirror allocations are excluded.
Trading symbol for this instrument (e.g. AAPL, TSLA)
Amount invested in this instrument as a ratio of realized equity. Decimal fraction: 0.5 = 50%.
Current value of this instrument as a ratio of total equity. Decimal fraction: 0.5 = 50%.
Time interval of the returned candles. Matches the interval parameter from the request.
List of candle data grouped by instrument
Identifier to send back on withdrawal creation.
Machine-readable error code.
Human-readable error message.
The permission scope name.
The permission scope names that may be assigned to agent-portfolio user tokens.

The amount in USD deducted from the CALLER's account balance to copy-trade this agent-portfolio. This is NOT the agent-portfolio's own balance — the agent-portfolio receives a separate fixed virtual balance (returned as agentPortfolioVirtualBalance). Positions are mirrored proportionally: e.g. $2,000 with a $10,000 virtual balance = 20% position sizing.
A unique display name for the agent-portfolio (6-10 characters).
An optional description of the agent-portfolio's purpose or strategy.
A human-readable name for the user token provisioned with the agent-portfolio.

The set of permission scope names to grant to the provisioned user token. Available scopes: etoro-public:trade.real:read, etoro-public:trade.real:write, etoro-public:trade.demo:read, etoro-public:trade.demo:write.
An optional set of IPv4 addresses allowed to use the provisioned user token.
An optional expiration date and time (UTC) for the provisioned user token.
The unique identifier of the newly created agent-portfolio.
The GCID associated with the agent-portfolio.
The fixed virtual balance (in USD) that the agent-portfolio was funded with. The investmentAmountInUsd used to copy is proportional to this balance.
The Trading API mirror ID for this agent-portfolio's copy trade.
The user tokens generated during agent-portfolio creation.
The unique identifier of the newly created user token.
The generated user token secret. Only available at creation time.
The OAuth client identifier associated with the user token.
The collection of agent-portfolios owned by the user.
When this agent-portfolio was created.
The name of the external application registered for this token.

[DEPRECATED — use scopeNames instead] The set of permission scope identifiers granted to this token. Available scopes: 200 = etoro-public:real:read, 201 = etoro-public:demo:read, 202 = etoro-public:real:write, 203 = etoro-public:demo:write.
Always false — indicates that the user token was not created.
Unique identifier of the financial instrument
Type of the financial instrument (e.g., 'Instrument', 'Person')
Ranking position of the item in the watchlist
Reason the item was added to the watchlist
Date and time the item was added
Market metadata for the instrument when included
Unique identifier of the watchlist
Global Customer ID of the watchlist owner
Type of the watchlist
Total number of items in the watchlist
Whether this is a default system watchlist
Whether this is the user's selected default watchlist
Display order rank of the watchlist
URL for dynamic watchlist queries
Items contained in the watchlist
Related asset IDs
List of user watchlists
Exception details when the request partially failed
Response metadata including pagination info
Whether the request succeeded
List of curated investment lists
Unique identifier of the curated list
Description of the curated list
URL of the list's image
Type of recommendation response
List of recommended instrument IDs
Container for all portfolio-related information
List of currently open trading positions
Available trading balance in USD, representing funds available for new actions
Copy trading configurations and positions
List of pending orders
Active orders to open positions
Active orders to close positions
Active orders to close multiple positions
Bonus credit amount in USD in the account
Total unrealized profit and loss across all open positions in the portfolio
Currency ID of the account (1 = USD)
Stock-specific pending orders
Entry orders awaiting execution
Exit orders awaiting execution
Unique identifier for the position
Customer ID associated with the position
Timestamp when the position was opened in ISO 8601 format
Entry price of the position in the instrument's currency
Identifier of the traded instrument
Mirror ID if the position is part of copy trading, 0 otherwise
Parent position ID for mirrored positions, 0 otherwise
Position direction: true for long (buy) positions, false for short (sell) positions
Rate at which TakeProfit will trigger and send MarketOrder to close the position
Rate at which StopLoss will trigger and send MarketOrder to close the position
USD amount allocated to the position. This amount includes both the initial investment, and additional margin allocated to the position as collateral
Leverage multiplier applied to the position
Original orderID the position was opened by. Need to match together with orderType
Original orderType of the order the position was opened by. Need to match together with orderId
Number of units in the position
Total overnight fees and dividends charged/paid on the position in USD. Negative amount represents refund
Initial investment USD amount in the position. This value does not change in case the position was partially closed
Indication if TrailingStopLoss feature is active on this position
Manual stop loss edit version. Each time StopLossRate is manually update this value is incremented
Obsolete
If the position is currently in redeem process, this value represents the current status
Initial invested units in the position. This value does not change in case the position was partially closed
Indication whether this position was partially closed
USD value of the current units in the position, based on the initial investment. If the position was not partially altered, this value equals initialAmountInDollars
Obsolete. This value is used to indicate if the relevant prices for the position are Ask/Bid or AskDiscounted/BidDiscounted
Position open reason
Position investment type. 0 - CFD, 1 - Real Asset, 2 - SWAP, 3 - Crypto MarginTrade, 4 - Future Contract
Indication if the position was originally opened inside a mirror and detached from it
Conversion rate from the asset currency to USD at the time the position was opened
Pnl formula used for calculating profit and loss
Total fees in USD charged on the position. Example - TicketFee. This value does not include overnight fees and dividends
Total taxes in USD charged on the position. Example - SDRT
Indication if TakeProfit is enabled for the position. false = enabled, true = disabled
Indication if StopLoss is enabled for the position. false = enabled, true = disabled
Number of lots the position represents. For FutureContracts, this value represents the number of contracts acquired. This property is not relevant for instruments that are not futures instruments
Customer ID of the trader being copied

The precentage of the mirror value that the StopLossAmount represented at the time of the last edit. Adding or removing funds from the mirror will trigger recalculation of StopLossAmount based on this value compared to the current mirror value
Indication if the mirror is currently paused, restricting open of additional positions inside the mirror
Indication if mirror originally copied all parent existing position on mirror registration
Available to trade USD balance in the mirror. This balance is reserved for mirror operations

USD value of the mirror at which MirrorStopLoss will be triggered and cause liquidation of the mirror. Adding or removing funds from the mirror will trigger recalculation of this value based on StopLossPercentage compared to the current mirror value
USD amount initially invested in the mirror
Total USD amount deposited into the mirror after initial investment
Total USD amount withdrawn from the mirror
Username of the trader being copied
Total USD net profit of all positions that closed in the mirror
Date and time when the mirror trading was initiated
Indication if the mirror is in closure process
Parent mirrors for this mirror (if any)
(Obsolete) Mirror positions weights calculation methodology
Current status of the mirror. 0 - Active, 1 - Paused, 2 - Pending Closure, 3 - In Alignment Process
Delayed orders for closing positions
Delayed orders for opening positions
Unique identifier for the order
Customer ID associated with the order
Asset rate at which to send market order to the market
Type of order execution
Status of the order
The last update timestamp of the order.
The number of units to trade.
Amount frozen for the order.
Total external costs associated with the trade.
External operation details, if any.
The number of units closed in this order.
The number of lots closed in this order.
The ID of the closed position.
IDs of positions pending close in this order.
Tracking token for the request, used for correlation and debugging purposes. This token is generated by the system and can be used to track the request through various system components.
Reference tracking ID for the order request.

Current status of the order. Common values: 0 = Pending, 1 = Executed, 2 = Cancelled, 3 = Rejected, 4 = Partially Executed. The exact meaning of status codes may vary based on order type and system configuration.
The action type that triggered the order creation. This indicates the reason or context for opening the position, such as manual trade, copy trading, automated strategy, etc.
The timestamp when the order request was initially created and submitted to the system. This is in ISO 8601 format (UTC).
The exact timestamp when this position was opened and executed. This is in ISO 8601 format (UTC) and represents when the position became active in the trading system.

The currency conversion rate that was applied when opening the position. This rate is used to convert between the instrument's base currency and the account currency (typically USD) at the time of execution.
Indicates whether the position is currently open (true) or has been closed (false). This status reflects the current state of the position at the time the order information was retrieved.
The account identifier associated with this order.
The portfolio identifier.
The order action. Possible values: open, close.
The transaction direction. Possible values: buy, sell, sellShort, buyToCover.
The internal eToro order type identifier.
The currency used for the order.
The requested monetary amount for the order.
The requested number of units for the order.
The requested number of contracts for the order.
The trigger rate for limit or stop orders.
The stop-loss rate at order open.
The take-profit rate at order open.
The stop-loss type. Possible values: fixed, trailing.
Total costs associated with the order.
List of position IDs to close as part of a close order.
List of position executions resulting from this order.
The timestamp when the order was requested.
The request sizing type. Possible values: byAmount, byUnits, byContracts.
Status identifier. Common values: 1 = Executed, 2 = Cancelled, 3 = Rejected.
The asset's base currency.
Settlement type. Possible values: cfd, real, realFutures, marginTrade.
The position side. Possible values: long, short.
The current state of the position. Possible values: open, closed.
The invested amount in the account currency.
The initial exposure in the account currency.
The initial exposure in the asset currency.
Additional funds added to the position.
Margin held in the account currency.
Margin held in the asset currency.
Remaining units in the position.
Remaining contracts in the position.
The timestamp when the position was opened.
The execution timestamp.
Number of contracts executed.
The average execution price.
The average currency conversion rate applied.
The market spread at execution time.
The markup applied to the spread.
The price snapshot identifier used for execution.
Fees charged for the execution.
Taxes applied to the execution.
The trigger rate for mit orders. Required for mit orders.
Additional margin to allocate to the position.
List of position IDs to close. Required for close orders.
The client reference identifier for the order, matching the X-Request-Id header if provided.
Cost components that would apply to the proposed order.
Timestamp (ISO 8601) at which the cost figures were generated.
Identifies which cost component this entry represents.
ISO 4217 code of the account's base currency (e.g. 'USD').
Positions held directly (not via copy trading), grouped by instrument.
Cash available for new trades: accountBalance minus accountFrozenCash.
Cash reserved for pending open orders.
Unrealized P&L across all manual positions and copy-trading mirrors in account currency.
Total portfolio value: accountAvailableCash + accountTotalUsedMargin + accountCurrentPnl.
Total margin in use: manual position margins + frozen order amounts + mirror active margins.
Total cash balance (available + frozen), excluding invested amounts.
ISO 4217 code of the instrument's base currency (e.g. 'USD', 'EUR').
Sum of margins across all positions for this instrument, in account currency.
Sum of transaction fees taken on positions of this instrument, in account currency.
Sum of taxes for this instrument, in asset currency.
Sum of taxes for this instrument, in account currency.
Sum of margins across all positions for this instrument, in asset currency.
Unrealized P&L for this instrument in asset currency. Null when P&L calculation is not requested.
Return on equity in account currency: accountCurrencyReturn / totalMarginAccountCurrency.
Net contracts across all positions (positive = net long, negative = net short).
Net units across all positions (positive = net long, negative = net short).
Net current market exposure in asset currency.
Net current market exposure in account currency.
Net initial exposure at open in account currency.
Unrealized P&L for this instrument in account currency.
Current liquidation value in account currency: totalMarginAccountCurrency + accountCurrencyReturn.
Current liquidation value in asset currency.
Average leverage across all positions for this instrument.
Weighted average open rate across all positions for this instrument.
Direction-aware weighted average open rate (long contributions minus short).
Cash available within this mirror for new copy positions.
Total amount ever deposited into this mirror: initialInvestment + depositSummary.
Total amount ever withdrawn from this mirror.
Stop-loss threshold as a percentage of the mirror's current value.
Stop-loss threshold in account currency. The mirror liquidates when its value falls to this level.
Accumulated net profit from all closed positions within this mirror.
Net amount funded into the mirror: mirrorDepositTotal minus mirrorWithdrawalTotal.
Total P&L from open and closed positions within the mirror, in account currency.
Current mirror value: mirrorActiveMargin + mirrorPositionsPnl.
mirrorPositionsPnl as a percentage of mirrorLiquidationValue.
This mirror's active margin as a percentage of the account's total used margin.
This mirror's liquidation value as a percentage of the account's total value.
Total margin in active use within this mirror: mirrorAvailableCash + sum of position margins minus closed positions P&L.
Optional list of instrument IDs to check.
Optional list of instrument symbols to check.
Trading configuration for each resolved instrument.
Instrument IDs that were requested but could not be found.
Symbols that were requested but could not be found.
Minimum exposure value required to open a position on this instrument.
Maximum number of units allowed per single order.
Whether opening new positions is currently permitted.
Whether closing existing positions is currently permitted.
Whether partially closing an existing position is permitted.
Whether Market-if-Touched (limit) orders are supported.
Whether submitting open orders when the market is closed is allowed.
Whether submitting close orders when the market is closed is allowed.
Whether a trailing stop-loss can be set on positions for this instrument.
Whether a W-8BEN tax form is required to trade this instrument. Null if not applicable.
What trade quantity type is allowed. Possible values: `WholeUnits`, `FractionalUnits`.
How orders are filled for this instrument. Possible values: `BestEffort`, `FillOrKill`.
How order size can be specified. Possible values: `Units`, `Amount`, `Both`.
Unit type used to express trade size. Possible values: `Units`, `Lots`.
Initial margin expressed in the asset currency. Null if not applicable.
Stop-loss margin expressed in the asset currency. Null if not applicable.
Additional buffer applied to the current rate to limit the order execution price - usually for low-liquidity assets. This also causes additional funds to be reserved for the order. Null if no buffer.
Available leverage configurations, each specific to a settlement type and direction.
The trade direction. Possible values: LONG, SHORT.
Available leverage multipliers for this settlement type and direction.
Additional user questionnaire may be required to allow the user to trade with this setup of settlement and leverages.
Minimum monetary collateral required to open a position under this leverage configuration.
Whether the stop-loss can be edited for positions under this configuration.
Minimum stop-loss percentage allowed from the allocated margin of the position.
Maximum stop-loss percentage allowed from the allocated margin of the position.
Default stop-loss percentage applied when no explicit value is provided.
Whether the take-profit can be edited.
Minimum take-profit percentage allowed from the allocated margin of the position.
Maximum take-profit percentage allowed from the allocated margin of the position.
Default take-profit percentage applied when no explicit value is provided.
Whether stop-loss and take-profit can be set on a position.
The current page number.
The number of items per page.
The display name of the instrument.
The number of unique users who viewed this instrument in the last 7 days.
The type ID of the instrument.
The type of the instrument.
The ID of the exchange where the instrument is traded.
The internal asset class ID.
The internal display name of the instrument.
Indicates whether the instrument is internal.
The full internal symbol of the instrument.
Indicates whether the instrument is hidden from clients.
The internal instrument ID.
The internal crypto type ID.
The internal exchange ID.
The internal exchange name.
The internal asset class name.
The URL of the 35x35 logo.
The URL of the 50x50 logo.
The URL of the 150x150 logo.
The daily price change.
The absolute daily price change.
The weekly price change.
The monthly price change.
Indicates whether the instrument is delisted.
Indicates whether the instrument is currently tradable.
Indicates whether the exchange is open.
The internal closing price.
Indicates whether the instrument is active in the platform.
Indicates whether buying is enabled for the instrument.
The current rate of the instrument.
The three-month price change.
The six-month price change.
The one-year price change.
The current month price change.
The current quarter price change.
The current year price change.
The last year price change.
The last two years price change.
The price change from one month ago.
The price change from two months ago.
The price change from three months ago.
The price change from six months ago.
The price change from one year ago.
The converted bid price.
The converted ask price.
The converted bid price without spread.
The converted ask price without spread.
The change in the number of traders over the last 7 days.
The change in the number of traders over the last 14 days.
The change in the number of traders over the last 30 days.
The number of unique users who viewed this instrument in the last 14 days.
The number of unique users who viewed this instrument in the last 30 days.
The internal industry ID.
The internal stock industry name.
Total number of unique users interested in this instrument.
The holding percentage of this instrument.
The buy holding percentage.
The sell holding percentage.
The buy percentage change in the last 24 hours.
The absolute buy percentage change in the last 24 hours.
The industry name ID.
The sector name ID.
Array of current market rates for requested instruments
List of cash accounts
User's creation eligibility status for cash accounts
Bank account linked to this cash account
Payment cards linked to this cash account
Identifier value as returned by the bank.
Name on the bank account
List of bank identifiers represented as key/value entries. Identifier type availability is bank and country specific.
Region of the bank account
ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code
Banking provider name
Name of the banking institution
Masked card number
Whether this is a virtual card
Date and time the card was created
Card expiration date
Client-generated idempotency key
Present only when status is Success
Real account Customer ID - the identifier for the user's real trading account.
Demo account Customer ID - the identifier for the user's virtual/demo trading account.
Player level. Possible values: 1 (Bronze), 2 (Platinum), 3 (Gold), 4 (Internal), 5 (Silver), 6 (PlatinumPlus), 7 (Diamond).
Gender. Possible values: 0 (Unknown), 1 (Male), 2 (Female).
Date of birth.
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI), when applicable.
Sum of absolute exposure across all instruments as a ratio of total daily investment. Decimal fraction: 0.5 = 50%.
Per-instrument exposure breakdown
Net exposure for this instrument as a ratio of total equity. Signed - positive = long, negative = short. Decimal fraction: 0.5 = 50%.
Host domain of the attachment
Type of media
Media content details

Number of direct replies to this comment
Inline reply preview. Each entry has the same shape as Comment; nested `replies` arrays are not populated at this level.

Updated text content

Parent entity reference

Last update timestamp

Market logo images

Numeric asset type ID

True when the post has been soft-deleted

Why this post appears in the feed (ForYou only). May be null or an object: { type: 'followed-user'|'instrument-trending', sourceId, owner }
Pagination info
List of comment/reply items
Required. ID of the post being shared.

Optional. Only set this when `sharedOriginDiscussion` itself points to a share — it identifies the intermediate share, so the created Post's `metadata.share.sharedPost` is set to this post and `metadata.share.sharedOriginPost` to `sharedOriginDiscussion`, collapsing the chain to a 2-level reference in subsequent reads. Leave unset for first-level shares.
List of share entries
Poll configuration
Unique identifier of the price alert
Target price at which the alert will trigger
Market bid price at the time the alert was created or last updated

Individual account balances.
Account sub-type, where applicable.
Account balance in the account's native currency.

Exchange rate applied to convert balance to displayCurrency.
Available balance or buying power (Trading, Options, MoneyFarm, Cash).

End of the returned date range (inclusive, ISO 8601).
End-of-day balance snapshots, one entry per day in the requested range.

Total cash across all accounts in native currency.
